data.body: Keep those summer vibes rolling on Governors Island and experience epic views of New York Harbor and Lady Liberty. Your entry includes endless sets of warm summer breezes, ferry rides to and from the island for you and your crew, an official race tech shirt, and our classic post-race eats. Race entries are not endless, so make sure to register soon.
data.title: NYCRUNS Endless Summer 10K

data.body: Now in its 15th year, the NYCRUNS Haunted Island 10K is NYC's most popular Halloween running event! Run in costume (as many do) or run as yourself, but whatever you do, register sooner rather than later because this race is destined to sell out (as it does every year). No tricks here, just treats. From the Halloween decorations to the costume contest, it's a spooktacular time! Entry includes a ferry ride for you and your guests, your race-entry, an official race shirt, and tasty post-race eats (yes, including Halloween candy).
data.title: NYCRUNS Haunted Island 10K

data.body: Treat yourself to a running tour around Governors Island, and we'll treat you to a well-deserved post-run ice cream (or ice pop, for those who so choose). Talk about a perfect summer day! Join us for the 5th Annual Manhattan Ice Cream Social 10K and you'll get a USATF-certified course with fluid stations and medical support, ferry rides to and from the island for you and your guests, an official race shirt, and some great post-race eats (including the aforementioned icy treat!).
data.title: NYCRUNS Manhattan Ice Cream Social 10K

data.body:
Misipasta is excited to announce Misipasta x Governors Island, powered by Resy, returns for the 2026 season. Misipasta will host dinners four nights a week for 16 guests a night, where diners can enjoy a unique version of an Italian countryside picnic while basking in some of the best sunset views in New York City.

Guests will be picked up at Pier 25 in Tribeca and will be greeted by Misipasta on Governors Island. All guests will then be transported back to Pier 25 following dessert.
data.title: Misipasta x Governors Island

data.body: Celebrate the past, present, and future of jazz music at the 6th Annual Jazz By The Water™ Concert Series on Governors Island! This event features top artists from around the world, as well as local talent, in a beautiful outdoor venue. Join for a weekend of music, food, and fun! Concerts are free and open to all.
data.title: Jazz by the Water™

data.body:
Porch Stomp invites dozens of folk bands, artists, and performing arts organizations to use the historic porches of Nolan Park as a space for performance and education, highlighting artistry ranging from traditional folk to old-time, blues, roots, bluegrass and Americana.

Porch Stomp is a free, volunteer-run music celebration produced in association with Make Music New York, a not-for-profit that connects music makers, fans and the public spaces of New York City. Porch Stomp offers unique performance and educational opportunities, including concerts, workshops and open jams. Each “stage” or porch is curated and produced by an arts organization or local venue that supports traditional arts, and will represent a specific folk community in New York City.
data.title: Porch Stomp

data.body: VW Gridlock, the epic vintage Volkswagen show, returns to Governors Island August 16! A collection of unique, vintage Volkswagens from across the years, including iconic models like the Beetle, Bus, Karmann Ghia, and Thing, will be parked at Picnic Point all day for visitors to enjoy. Free and open to all.
data.title: Gridlock on Governors Island

data.body:
Amer­i­can Indi­an Com­mu­ni­ty House (AICH) is a com­mu­ni­ty-based orga­ni­za­tion with a mis­sion to improve and pro­mote the well-being of the Amer­i­can Indi­an Com­mu­ni­ty and to increase the vis­i­bil­i­ty of Amer­i­can Indi­an cul­tures in an urban set­ting. Their space on Gov­er­nors Island is used to present exhi­bi­tions, per­for­mances, and oth­er cul­tur­al and edu­ca­tion­al pro­grams, as well as to host artists in res­i­dence. AICH was found­ed in 1969 by Native Amer­i­can vol­un­teers as a com­mu­ni­ty-based orga­ni­za­tion, man­dat­ed to improve the sta­tus of Native Amer­i­cans, and to fos­ter inter-cul­tur­al under­stand­ing. AICH mem­ber­ship is cur­rent­ly com­posed of Native Amer­i­cans from 72 dif­fer­ent tribes. Native Amer­i­can migra­tion between urban cen­ters and reser­va­tions demon­strates the inter-relat­ed­ness of all Native Amer­i­cans, and from this real­i­ty emerges the recog­ni­tion that our issues and con­cerns are tru­ly shared.

This year's AICH Governors Island Indigenous Artist Residency seeks to uplift Native and Indigenous artists based in New York City. The residency provides a space for artists to focus on their work for two months; resident artists will host one workshop per month available to the general public.
data.title: American Indian Community House

data.body:
Founded as a not-for-profit organization by artists in 1977, Harvestworks has helped a generation of artists create new works using technology through their mission to support the creation and presentation of art works achieved using new and evolving technologies. The Harvestworks Art and Technology Program on Governors Island is centered on art works created at the intersection of art and technology. Harvestworks presents digital media and code-based art exhibitions, public workshops and artist talks. The program provides exhibition opportunities to contemporary electronic media artists and educates the public about new technology and how artists use it for artistic expression.

Harvestworks' return to Governors Island this season will foster collaboration across disciplines to present cutting-edge work at the center of art, science, and technology. This year's artists in residence will develop and present experimental projects in electronic and digital art.
data.title: Harvestworks

data.body: Based on Governors Island, Billion Oyster Project is restoring oyster populations to New York Harbor in collaboration with NYC communities and through public education initiatives, in turn improving water quality, fostering new marine habitats, and creating greater coastal resilience. Throughout this season, Bil­lion Oys­ter Project’s house will hold an oys­ter dis­play, invit­ing vis­i­tors to learn more about ecosys­tems and these engi­neers, their impor­tance to New York Har­bor, and how every­one can get involved.
data.title: Billion Oyster Project

data.body:
Governors Island offers 7 miles of car free biking with incredible views!

Located just a short walk from both ferry landings and open every day from 9am - 5:30pm, Blazing Saddles is a great way to have fun and explore everything the Island has to offer. Their fleet of 1,000 bikes has something for everyone, including kids’ equipment, pedal cars, and surreys for 2-6 riders. Reservations are not necessary, and their staff will help you get going on a great ride. For rental questions for groups of 10 more, please email newyork@blazingsaddles.com
data.title: Blazing Saddles Bike Rentals

data.body:
Slide Hill has reopened after undergoing planned maintenance.

Zoom down the hillside at Slide Hill! Located in the beautiful parkland on the southern half of Governors Island, Slide Hill offers a variety of slides that are bound to delight young visitors and families. Scramble up the Timber Climb or take a winding path up the hill and fly back down! Slide Hill features three shorter slides, including a family slide built for two people to ride down at once. Thrill-seekers will get a rush on the fourth slide: a curving, 57 foot-long, three story-high slide—the longest in NYC!
data.title: Slide Hill

data.body:
Head to Hammock Grove to relax and swing in the Island's iconic red hammocks, open year-round. Kick back in peaceful nooks among this young urban forest or explore our tucked away walking paths. You may even see the Statue of Liberty and the Lower Manhattan skyline peeking over you!

Please note: Broken and/or older hammocks are currently being replaced in preparation for the summer season.
data.title: Hammock Grove

data.body: The Hammock Grove Play Area features play structures that kids will love to climb and swing on to their hearts’ content, located on the south end of Liggett Terrace.
data.title: Hammock Grove Play Area

data.body:
Rachel Whiteread’s installation Cabin continues the artist’s interest in producing evocative sculptures of negative spaces and structures. In this case, the artist has sited a concrete cast of the interior of a simple cabin, a place suggestive of retreat and introspection. Sitting modestly on the hillside of Discovery Hill overlooking New York Harbor, the sculpture creates a sense of contemplative quiet and distance from the bustle of the city across the water. Strewn around the cabin are numerous bronze casts of discarded objects including bottles, cans and other refuse; some of these objects were sourced on the Island itself.

Governors Island Arts presents its program with the visionary support of the Ford Foundation, as well the Mellon Foundation, the Charina Endowment Fund, Donald R. Mullen Family Foundation, Stavros Niarchos Foundation, Bloomberg Philanthropies, The Gottesman Fund, Donald A. Pels Charitable Trust, the National Endowment for the Arts, and the New York State Council on the Arts with the support of the Office of the Governor and the New York State Legislature.
data.title: Cabin
data.subtitle: By Rachel Whiteread

data.body:
Commissioned in 2013 as part of the project, Sidewalk Island, Mark Handforth's Yankee Hanger is sited a short walk from Yankee Pier in Liggett Terrace. This playful, snaking sculpture invites visitors to sit on, walk through, and engage with it as it wistfully towers above the landscape.

Governors Island Arts presents its program with the visionary support of the Ford Foundation, as well the Mellon Foundation, the Charina Endowment Fund, Donald R. Mullen Family Foundation, Stavros Niarchos Foundation, Bloomberg Philanthropies, The Gottesman Fund, Donald A. Pels Charitable Trust, the National Endowment for the Arts, and the New York State Council on the Arts with the support of the Office of the Governor and the New York State Legislature.
data.title: Yankee Hanger
data.subtitle: by Mark Handforth
